types.name: Annotations
types.description: Optional annotations for the client. The client can use annotations to inform how objects are used or displayed

fields.name: audience
fields.description:
Describes who the intended audience of this object or data is.

It can include multiple entries to indicate content useful for multiple audiences (e.g., `["user", "assistant"]`).

fields.name: lastModified
fields.description:
The moment the resource was last modified, as an ISO 8601 formatted string.

Should be an ISO 8601 formatted string (e.g., "2025-01-12T15:00:58Z").

Examples: last activity timestamp in an open file, timestamp when the resource
was attached, etc.

fields.name: priority
fields.description:
Describes how important this data is for operating the server.

A value of 1 means "most important," and indicates that the data is
effectively required, while 0 means "least important," and indicates that
the data is entirely optional.

types.name: AudioContent
types.description: Audio provided to or from an LLM.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

fields.name: data
fields.description: The base64-encoded audio data.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type of the audio. Different providers may support different audio types.

types.name: BaseMetadata
types.description: Base interface for metadata with name (identifier) and title (display name) properties.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for {@link Tool},
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

fields.name: blob
fields.description: A base64-encoded string representing the binary data of the item.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type of this resource, if known.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of this resource.

types.name: CacheableResult
types.description: A result that supports a time-to-live (TTL) hint for client-side caching.

fields.name: cacheScope
fields.description:
Indicates the intended scope of the cached response, analogous to HTTP
`Cache-Control: public` vs `Cache-Control: private`.

- `"public"`: The response does not contain user-specific data. Any
  client or intermediary (e.g., shared gateway, caching proxy) MAY cache
  the response and serve it across authorization contexts.
- `"private"`: The response MAY be cached and reused only within the
  same authorization context. Caches MUST NOT be shared across
  authorization contexts (e.g., a different access token requires a
  different cache).

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

fields.name: ttlMs
fields.description:
A hint from the server indicating how long (in milliseconds) the
client MAY cache this response before re-fetching. Semantics are
analogous to HTTP Cache-Control max-age.

- If 0, The response SHOULD be considered immediately stale,
  The client MAY re-fetch every time the result is needed.
- If positive, the client SHOULD consider the result fresh for this many
  milliseconds after receiving the response.

types.name: CallToolRequest
types.description: Used by the client to invoke a tool provided by the server.

types.name: CallToolRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `tools/call` request.

fields.name: arguments
fields.description: Arguments to use for the tool call.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the tool.

types.name: CallToolResult
types.description: The result returned by the server for a {@link CallToolRequesttools/call} request.

fields.name: content
fields.description: A list of content objects that represent the unstructured result of the tool call.

fields.name: isError
fields.description:
Whether the tool call ended in an error.

If not set, this is assumed to be false (the call was successful).

Any errors that originate from the tool SHOULD be reported inside the result
object, with `isError` set to true, _not_ as an MCP protocol-level error
response. Otherwise, the LLM would not be able to see that an error occurred
and self-correct.

However, any errors in _finding_ the tool, an error indicating that the
server does not support tool calls, or any other exceptional conditions,
should be reported as an MCP error response.

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

fields.name: structuredContent
fields.description:
An optional JSON value that represents the structured result of the tool call.

This can be any JSON value (object, array, string, number, boolean, or null)
that conforms to the tool's outputSchema if one is defined.

types.name: CallToolResultResponse
types.description: A successful response from the server for a {@link CallToolRequesttools/call} request.

types.name: CancelledNotification
types.description:
This notification is sent by the client to indicate that it is cancelling a request it previously issued.

On stdio, the server also sends this notification, solely to terminate a {@link SubscriptionsListenRequestsubscriptions/listen} stream: it references the ID of the `subscriptions/listen` request that opened the stream. Servers MUST NOT use this notification to cancel any other request.

The request SHOULD still be in-flight, but due to communication latency, it is always possible that this notification MAY arrive after the request has already finished.

This notification indicates that the result will be unused, so any associated processing SHOULD cease.

types.name: CancelledNotificationParams
types.description: Parameters for a `notifications/cancelled` notification.

fields.name: reason
fields.description: An optional string describing the reason for the cancellation. This MAY be logged or presented to the user.

fields.name: requestId
fields.description:
The ID of the request to cancel.

This MUST correspond to the ID of a request the client previously issued.

types.name: ClientCapabilities
types.description: Capabilities a client may support. Known capabilities are defined here, in this schema, but this is not a closed set: any client can define its own, additional capabilities.

fields.name: elicitation
fields.description: Present if the client supports elicitation from the server.

fields.name: experimental
fields.description: Experimental, non-standard capabilities that the client supports.

fields.name: extensions
fields.description:
Optional MCP extensions that the client supports. Keys are extension identifiers
(e.g., "io.modelcontextprotocol/oauth-client-credentials"), and values are
per-extension settings objects. An empty object indicates support with no settings.

Keys MUST follow the {@link MetaObject`_meta` key naming rules}, with a
mandatory prefix.

fields.name: roots
fields.description: Present if the client supports listing roots.

fields.name: sampling
context
fields.description:
Whether the client supports context inclusion via `includeContext` parameter.
If not declared, servers SHOULD only use `includeContext: "none"` (or omit it).

fields.name: tools
fields.description: Whether the client supports tool use via `tools` and `toolChoice` parameters.
Present if the client supports sampling from an LLM.

types.name: ClientNotification
types.description:
This notification is sent by the client to indicate that it is cancelling a request it previously issued.

On stdio, the server also sends this notification, solely to terminate a {@link SubscriptionsListenRequestsubscriptions/listen} stream: it references the ID of the `subscriptions/listen` request that opened the stream. Servers MUST NOT use this notification to cancel any other request.

The request SHOULD still be in-flight, but due to communication latency, it is always possible that this notification MAY arrive after the request has already finished.

This notification indicates that the result will be unused, so any associated processing SHOULD cease.

types.name: ClientResult
types.description: Common result fields.

types.name: CompleteRequest
types.description: A request from the client to the server, to ask for completion options.

types.name: CompleteRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `completion/complete` request.

fields.name: argument
name
fields.description: The name of the argument

fields.name: value
fields.description: The value of the argument to use for completion matching.
The argument's information

fields.name: context
arguments
fields.description: Previously-resolved variables in a URI template or prompt.
Additional, optional context for completions

types.name: CompleteResult
types.description: The result returned by the server for a {@link CompleteRequestcompletion/complete} request.

fields.name: hasMore
fields.description: Indicates whether there are additional completion options beyond those provided in the current response, even if the exact total is unknown.

fields.name: total
fields.description: The total number of completion options available. This can exceed the number of values actually sent in the response.

fields.name: values
fields.description: An array of completion values. Must not exceed 100 items.

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

types.name: CompleteResultResponse
types.description: A successful response from the server for a {@link CompleteRequestcompletion/complete} request.

types.name: CreateMessageRequest
types.description: A request from the server to sample an LLM via the client. The client has full discretion over which model to select. The client should also inform the user before beginning sampling, to allow them to inspect the request (human in the loop) and decide whether to approve it.

types.name: CreateMessageRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `sampling/createMessage` request.

fields.name: includeContext
fields.description:
A request to include context from one or more MCP servers (including the caller), to be attached to the prompt.
The client MAY ignore this request.

Default is `"none"`. The values `"thisServer"` and `"allServers"` are deprecated (SEP-2596): servers SHOULD
omit this field or use `"none"`, and SHOULD only use the deprecated values if the client declares
{@link ClientCapabilities.sampling.context}.

fields.name: maxTokens
fields.description:
The requested maximum number of tokens to sample (to prevent runaway completions).

The client MAY choose to sample fewer tokens than the requested maximum.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Optional metadata to pass through to the LLM provider. The format of this metadata is provider-specific.

fields.name: modelPreferences
fields.description: The server's preferences for which model to select. The client MAY ignore these preferences.

fields.name: systemPrompt
fields.description: An optional system prompt the server wants to use for sampling. The client MAY modify or omit this prompt.

fields.name: toolChoice
fields.description:
Controls how the model uses tools.
The client MUST return an error if this field is provided but {@link ClientCapabilities.sampling.tools} is not declared.
Default is `{ mode: "auto" }`.

fields.name: tools
fields.description:
Tools that the model may use during generation.
The client MUST return an error if this field is provided but {@link ClientCapabilities.sampling.tools} is not declared.

types.name: CreateMessageResult
types.description:
The result returned by the client for a {@link CreateMessageRequestsampling/createMessage} request.
The client should inform the user before returning the sampled message, to allow them
to inspect the response (human in the loop) and decide whether to allow the server to see it.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The name of the model that generated the message.

fields.name: stopReason
fields.description:
The reason why sampling stopped, if known.

Standard values:
- `"endTurn"`: Natural end of the assistant's turn
- `"stopSequence"`: A stop sequence was encountered
- `"maxTokens"`: Maximum token limit was reached
- `"toolUse"`: The model wants to use one or more tools

This field is an open string to allow for provider-specific stop reasons.

types.name: Cursor
types.description: An opaque token used to represent a cursor for pagination.

types.name: DiscoverRequest
types.description:
A request from the client asking the server to advertise its supported
protocol versions, capabilities, and other metadata. Servers **MUST**
implement `server/discover`. Clients **MAY** call it but are not required
to — version negotiation can also happen inline via per-request `_meta`.

types.name: DiscoverResult
types.description: The result returned by the server for a {@link DiscoverRequestserver/discover} request.

fields.name: cacheScope
fields.description:
Indicates the intended scope of the cached response, analogous to HTTP
`Cache-Control: public` vs `Cache-Control: private`.

- `"public"`: The response does not contain user-specific data. Any
  client or intermediary (e.g., shared gateway, caching proxy) MAY cache
  the response and serve it across authorization contexts.
- `"private"`: The response MAY be cached and reused only within the
  same authorization context. Caches MUST NOT be shared across
  authorization contexts (e.g., a different access token requires a
  different cache).

fields.name: capabilities
fields.description: The capabilities of the server.

fields.name: instructions
fields.description:
Natural-language guidance describing the server and its features.

This can be used by clients to improve an LLM's understanding of
available tools (e.g., by including it in a system prompt). It should
focus on information that helps the model use the server effectively
and should not duplicate information already in tool descriptions.

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

fields.name: supportedVersions
fields.description:
MCP Protocol Versions this server supports. The client should choose a
version from this list for use in subsequent requests.

fields.name: ttlMs
fields.description:
A hint from the server indicating how long (in milliseconds) the
client MAY cache this response before re-fetching. Semantics are
analogous to HTTP Cache-Control max-age.

- If 0, The response SHOULD be considered immediately stale,
  The client MAY re-fetch every time the result is needed.
- If positive, the client SHOULD consider the result fresh for this many
  milliseconds after receiving the response.

types.name: DiscoverResultResponse
types.description: A successful response from the server for a {@link DiscoverRequestserver/discover} request.

types.name: ElicitRequest
types.description: A request from the server to elicit additional information from the user via the client.

types.name: ElicitRequestFormParams
types.description: The parameters for a request to elicit non-sensitive information from the user via a form in the client.

fields.name: message
fields.description: The message to present to the user describing what information is being requested.

fields.name: mode
fields.description: The elicitation mode.

fields.name: requestedSchema
fields.description:
A restricted subset of JSON Schema.
Only top-level properties are allowed, without nesting.

types.name: ElicitRequestParams
types.description: The parameters for a request to elicit additional information from the user via the client.

types.name: ElicitRequestURLParams
types.description: The parameters for a request to elicit information from the user via a URL in the client.

fields.name: message
fields.description: The message to present to the user explaining why the interaction is needed.

fields.name: mode
fields.description: The elicitation mode.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL that the user should navigate to.

types.name: ElicitResult
types.description: The result returned by the client for an {@link ElicitRequestelicitation/create} request.

fields.name: action
fields.description:
The user action in response to the elicitation.
- `"accept"`: User submitted the form/confirmed the action
- `"decline"`: User explicitly declined the action
- `"cancel"`: User dismissed without making an explicit choice

fields.name: content
fields.description:
The submitted form data, only present when action is `"accept"` and mode was `"form"`.
Contains values matching the requested schema.
Omitted for out-of-band mode responses.

types.name: EmbeddedResource
types.description:
The contents of a resource, embedded into a prompt or tool call result.

It is up to the client how best to render embedded resources for the benefit
of the LLM and/or the user.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

types.name: EmptyResult
types.description: Common result fields.

fields.name: code
fields.description: The error type that occurred.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Additional information about the error. The value of this member is defined by the sender (e.g. detailed error information, nested errors etc.).

fields.name: message
fields.description: A short description of the error. The message SHOULD be limited to a concise single sentence.

types.name: GetPromptRequest
types.description: Used by the client to get a prompt provided by the server.

types.name: GetPromptRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `prompts/get` request.

fields.name: arguments
fields.description: Arguments to use for templating the prompt.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the prompt or prompt template.

types.name: GetPromptResult
types.description: The result returned by the server for a {@link GetPromptRequestprompts/get} request.

fields.name: description
fields.description: An optional description for the prompt.

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

types.name: GetPromptResultResponse
types.description: A successful response from the server for a {@link GetPromptRequestprompts/get} request.

types.name: HeaderMismatchError
types.description:
Returned when a server rejects a request because the values in the HTTP
headers do not match the corresponding values in the request body, or
because required headers are missing or malformed. For HTTP, the response
status code MUST be `400 Bad Request`.

types.name: Icon
types.description: An optionally-sized icon that can be displayed in a user interface.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description:
Optional MIME type override if the source MIME type is missing or generic.
For example: `"image/png"`, `"image/jpeg"`, or `"image/svg+xml"`.

fields.name: sizes
fields.description:
Optional array of strings that specify sizes at which the icon can be used.
Each string should be in WxH format (e.g., `"48x48"`, `"96x96"`) or `"any"` for scalable formats like SVG.

If not provided, the client should assume that the icon can be used at any size.

fields.name: src
fields.description:
A standard URI pointing to an icon resource. May be an HTTP/HTTPS URL or a
`data:` URI with Base64-encoded image data.

Consumers SHOULD take steps to ensure URLs serving icons are from the
same domain as the client/server or a trusted domain.

Consumers SHOULD take appropriate precautions when consuming SVGs as they can contain
executable JavaScript.

fields.name: theme
fields.description:
Optional specifier for the theme this icon is designed for. `"light"` indicates
the icon is designed to be used with a light background, and `"dark"` indicates
the icon is designed to be used with a dark background.

If not provided, the client should assume the icon can be used with any theme.

types.name: Icons
types.description: Base interface to add `icons` property.

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

types.name: ImageContent
types.description: An image provided to or from an LLM.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

fields.name: data
fields.description: The base64-encoded image data.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type of the image. Different providers may support different image types.

types.name: Implementation
types.description: Describes the MCP implementation.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
An optional human-readable description of what this implementation does.

This can be used by clients or servers to provide context about their purpose
and capabilities. For example, a server might describe the types of resources
or tools it provides, while a client might describe its intended use case.

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for {@link Tool},
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

fields.name: version
fields.description: The version of this implementation.

fields.name: websiteUrl
fields.description: An optional URL of the website for this implementation.

types.name: InputRequests
types.description:
A map of server-initiated requests that the client must fulfill.
Keys are server-assigned identifiers; values are the request objects.

types.name: InputRequiredResult
types.description:
An InputRequiredResult sent by the server to indicate that additional input is needed
before the request can be completed.

At least one of `inputRequests` or `requestState` MUST be present.

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

types.name: InputResponses
InternalError
types.description: A JSON-RPC error indicating that an internal error occurred on the receiver. This error is returned when the receiver encounters an unexpected condition that prevents it from fulfilling the request.

fields.name: code
fields.description: The error type that occurred.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Additional information about the error. The value of this member is defined by the sender (e.g. detailed error information, nested errors etc.).

fields.name: message
fields.description: A short description of the error. The message SHOULD be limited to a concise single sentence.

types.name: InvalidParamsError
types.description:
A JSON-RPC error indicating that the method parameters are invalid or malformed.

In MCP, this error is returned in various contexts when request parameters fail validation:

- **Tools**: Unknown tool name or invalid tool arguments
- **Prompts**: Unknown prompt name or missing required arguments
- **Pagination**: Invalid or expired cursor values
- **Logging**: Invalid log level
- **Elicitation**: Server requests an elicitation mode not declared in client capabilities
- **Sampling**: Missing tool result or tool results mixed with other content

fields.name: code
fields.description: The error type that occurred.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Additional information about the error. The value of this member is defined by the sender (e.g. detailed error information, nested errors etc.).

fields.name: message
fields.description: A short description of the error. The message SHOULD be limited to a concise single sentence.

types.name: InvalidRequestError
types.description: A JSON-RPC error indicating that the request is not a valid request object. This error is returned when the message structure does not conform to the JSON-RPC 2.0 specification requirements for a request (e.g., missing required fields like `jsonrpc` or `method`, or using invalid types for these fields).

fields.name: code
fields.description: The error type that occurred.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Additional information about the error. The value of this member is defined by the sender (e.g. detailed error information, nested errors etc.).

fields.name: message
fields.description: A short description of the error. The message SHOULD be limited to a concise single sentence.

types.name: JSONRPCErrorResponse
types.description: A response to a request that indicates an error occurred.

types.name: JSONRPCMessage
types.description: Refers to any valid JSON-RPC object that can be decoded off the wire, or encoded to be sent.

types.name: JSONRPCNotification
types.description: A notification which does not expect a response.

types.name: JSONRPCRequest
types.description: A request that expects a response.

types.name: JSONRPCResponse
types.description: A response to a request, containing either the result or error.

types.name: JSONRPCResultResponse
types.description: A successful (non-error) response to a request.

types.name: LegacyTitledEnumSchema
types.description:
Use {@link TitledSingleSelectEnumSchema} instead.
This interface will be removed in a future version.

fields.name: enumNames
fields.description:
(Legacy) Display names for enum values.
Non-standard according to JSON schema 2020-12.

types.name: ListPromptsRequest
types.description: Sent from the client to request a list of prompts and prompt templates the server has.

types.name: ListPromptsResult
types.description: The result returned by the server for a {@link ListPromptsRequestprompts/list} request.

fields.name: cacheScope
fields.description:
Indicates the intended scope of the cached response, analogous to HTTP
`Cache-Control: public` vs `Cache-Control: private`.

- `"public"`: The response does not contain user-specific data. Any
  client or intermediary (e.g., shared gateway, caching proxy) MAY cache
  the response and serve it across authorization contexts.
- `"private"`: The response MAY be cached and reused only within the
  same authorization context. Caches MUST NOT be shared across
  authorization contexts (e.g., a different access token requires a
  different cache).

fields.name: nextCursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
If present, there may be more results available.

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

fields.name: ttlMs
fields.description:
A hint from the server indicating how long (in milliseconds) the
client MAY cache this response before re-fetching. Semantics are
analogous to HTTP Cache-Control max-age.

- If 0, The response SHOULD be considered immediately stale,
  The client MAY re-fetch every time the result is needed.
- If positive, the client SHOULD consider the result fresh for this many
  milliseconds after receiving the response.

types.name: ListPromptsResultResponse
types.description: A successful response from the server for a {@link ListPromptsRequestprompts/list} request.

types.name: ListResourceTemplatesRequest
types.description: Sent from the client to request a list of resource templates the server has.

types.name: ListResourceTemplatesResult
types.description: The result returned by the server for a {@link ListResourceTemplatesRequestresources/templates/list} request.

fields.name: cacheScope
fields.description:
Indicates the intended scope of the cached response, analogous to HTTP
`Cache-Control: public` vs `Cache-Control: private`.

- `"public"`: The response does not contain user-specific data. Any
  client or intermediary (e.g., shared gateway, caching proxy) MAY cache
  the response and serve it across authorization contexts.
- `"private"`: The response MAY be cached and reused only within the
  same authorization context. Caches MUST NOT be shared across
  authorization contexts (e.g., a different access token requires a
  different cache).

fields.name: nextCursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
If present, there may be more results available.

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

fields.name: ttlMs
fields.description:
A hint from the server indicating how long (in milliseconds) the
client MAY cache this response before re-fetching. Semantics are
analogous to HTTP Cache-Control max-age.

- If 0, The response SHOULD be considered immediately stale,
  The client MAY re-fetch every time the result is needed.
- If positive, the client SHOULD consider the result fresh for this many
  milliseconds after receiving the response.

types.name: ListResourceTemplatesResultResponse
types.description: A successful response from the server for a {@link ListResourceTemplatesRequestresources/templates/list} request.

types.name: ListResourcesRequest
types.description: Sent from the client to request a list of resources the server has.

types.name: ListResourcesResult
types.description: The result returned by the server for a {@link ListResourcesRequestresources/list} request.

fields.name: cacheScope
fields.description:
Indicates the intended scope of the cached response, analogous to HTTP
`Cache-Control: public` vs `Cache-Control: private`.

- `"public"`: The response does not contain user-specific data. Any
  client or intermediary (e.g., shared gateway, caching proxy) MAY cache
  the response and serve it across authorization contexts.
- `"private"`: The response MAY be cached and reused only within the
  same authorization context. Caches MUST NOT be shared across
  authorization contexts (e.g., a different access token requires a
  different cache).

fields.name: nextCursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
If present, there may be more results available.

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

fields.name: ttlMs
fields.description:
A hint from the server indicating how long (in milliseconds) the
client MAY cache this response before re-fetching. Semantics are
analogous to HTTP Cache-Control max-age.

- If 0, The response SHOULD be considered immediately stale,
  The client MAY re-fetch every time the result is needed.
- If positive, the client SHOULD consider the result fresh for this many
  milliseconds after receiving the response.

types.name: ListResourcesResultResponse
types.description: A successful response from the server for a {@link ListResourcesRequestresources/list} request.

types.name: ListRootsRequest
types.description:
Sent from the server to request a list of root URIs from the client. Roots allow
servers to ask for specific directories or files to operate on. A common example
for roots is providing a set of repositories or directories a server should operate
on.

This request is typically used when the server needs to understand the file system
structure or access specific locations that the client has permission to read from.

types.name: ListRootsResult
types.description:
The result returned by the client for a {@link ListRootsRequestroots/list} request.
This result contains an array of {@link Root} objects, each representing a root directory
or file that the server can operate on.

types.name: ListToolsRequest
types.description: Sent from the client to request a list of tools the server has.

types.name: ListToolsResult
types.description: The result returned by the server for a {@link ListToolsRequesttools/list} request.

fields.name: cacheScope
fields.description:
Indicates the intended scope of the cached response, analogous to HTTP
`Cache-Control: public` vs `Cache-Control: private`.

- `"public"`: The response does not contain user-specific data. Any
  client or intermediary (e.g., shared gateway, caching proxy) MAY cache
  the response and serve it across authorization contexts.
- `"private"`: The response MAY be cached and reused only within the
  same authorization context. Caches MUST NOT be shared across
  authorization contexts (e.g., a different access token requires a
  different cache).

fields.name: nextCursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
If present, there may be more results available.

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

fields.name: ttlMs
fields.description:
A hint from the server indicating how long (in milliseconds) the
client MAY cache this response before re-fetching. Semantics are
analogous to HTTP Cache-Control max-age.

- If 0, The response SHOULD be considered immediately stale,
  The client MAY re-fetch every time the result is needed.
- If positive, the client SHOULD consider the result fresh for this many
  milliseconds after receiving the response.

types.name: ListToolsResultResponse
types.description: A successful response from the server for a {@link ListToolsRequesttools/list} request.

types.name: LoggingLevel
types.description:
The severity of a log message.

These map to syslog message severities, as specified in RFC-5424:
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5424#section-6.2.1

types.name: LoggingMessageNotification
types.description: JSONRPCNotification of a log message passed from server to client. The client opts in by setting `"io.modelcontextprotocol/logLevel"` in a request's `_meta`.

types.name: LoggingMessageNotificationParams
types.description: Parameters for a `notifications/message` notification.

fields.name: data
fields.description: The data to be logged, such as a string message or an object. Any JSON serializable type is allowed here.

fields.name: level
fields.description: The severity of this log message.

fields.name: logger
fields.description: An optional name of the logger issuing this message.

types.name: MetaObject
types.description:
Represents the contents of a `_meta` field, which clients and servers use to attach additional metadata to their interactions.

Certain key names are reserved by MCP for protocol-level metadata; implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at these keys. Additionally, specific schema definitions may reserve particular names for purpose-specific metadata, as declared in those definitions.

Valid keys have two segments:

**Prefix:**
- Optional — if specified, MUST be a series of _labels_ separated by dots (`.`), followed by a slash (`/`).
- Labels MUST start with a letter and end with a letter or digit. Interior characters may be letters, digits, or hyphens (`-`).
- Implementations SHOULD use reverse DNS notation (e.g., `com.example/` rather than `example.com/`).
- Any prefix where the second label is `modelcontextprotocol` or `mcp` is **reserved** for MCP use. For example: `io.modelcontextprotocol/`, `dev.mcp/`, `org.modelcontextprotocol.api/`, and `com.mcp.tools/` are all reserved. However, `com.example.mcp/` is NOT reserved, as the second label is `example`.

**Name:**
- Unless empty, MUST start and end with an alphanumeric character (`[a-z0-9A-Z]`).
- Interior characters may be alphanumeric, hyphens (`-`), underscores (`_`), or dots (`.`).

types.name: MethodNotFoundError
types.description:
A JSON-RPC error indicating that the requested method does not exist or is not available.

In MCP, a server returns this error when a client invokes a method the server does not implement — either a genuinely unknown method, or one gated behind a server capability the server did not advertise (e.g., calling `prompts/list` when the `prompts` capability was not advertised).

A request that requires a client capability the client did not declare is signalled instead by {@link MissingRequiredClientCapabilityError} (`-32021`).

fields.name: code
fields.description: The error type that occurred.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Additional information about the error. The value of this member is defined by the sender (e.g. detailed error information, nested errors etc.).

fields.name: message
fields.description: A short description of the error. The message SHOULD be limited to a concise single sentence.

types.name: MissingRequiredClientCapabilityError
types.description:
Returned when processing a request requires a capability the client did not
declare in `clientCapabilities`. For HTTP, the response status code MUST be
`400 Bad Request`.

fields.name: requiredCapabilities
fields.description: The capabilities the server requires from the client to process this request.

types.name: ModelHint
types.description:
Hints to use for model selection.

Keys not declared here are currently left unspecified by the spec and are up
to the client to interpret.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
A hint for a model name.

The client SHOULD treat this as a substring of a model name; for example:
 - `claude-3-5-sonnet` should match `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022`
 - `sonnet` should match `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022`, `claude-3-sonnet-20240229`, etc.
 - `claude` should match any Claude model

The client MAY also map the string to a different provider's model name or a different model family, as long as it fills a similar niche; for example:
 - `gemini-1.5-flash` could match `claude-3-haiku-20240307`

types.name: ModelPreferences
types.description:
The server's preferences for model selection, requested of the client during sampling.

Because LLMs can vary along multiple dimensions, choosing the "best" model is
rarely straightforward.  Different models excel in different areas—some are
faster but less capable, others are more capable but more expensive, and so
on. This interface allows servers to express their priorities across multiple
dimensions to help clients make an appropriate selection for their use case.

These preferences are always advisory. The client MAY ignore them. It is also
up to the client to decide how to interpret these preferences and how to
balance them against other considerations.

fields.name: costPriority
fields.description:
How much to prioritize cost when selecting a model. A value of 0 means cost
is not important, while a value of 1 means cost is the most important
factor.

fields.name: hints
fields.description:
Optional hints to use for model selection.

If multiple hints are specified, the client MUST evaluate them in order
(such that the first match is taken).

The client SHOULD prioritize these hints over the numeric priorities, but
MAY still use the priorities to select from ambiguous matches.

fields.name: intelligencePriority
fields.description:
How much to prioritize intelligence and capabilities when selecting a
model. A value of 0 means intelligence is not important, while a value of 1
means intelligence is the most important factor.

fields.name: speedPriority
fields.description:
How much to prioritize sampling speed (latency) when selecting a model. A
value of 0 means speed is not important, while a value of 1 means speed is
the most important factor.

types.name: NotificationMetaObject
types.description: Extends {@link MetaObject} with additional notification-specific fields. All key naming rules from `MetaObject` apply.

fields.name: io.modelcontextprotocol/subscriptionId
fields.description:
Identifies the subscription stream a notification was delivered on. The
server MUST include this key on every notification delivered via a
{@link SubscriptionsListenRequestsubscriptions/listen} stream, so the
client can correlate the notification with the originating subscription.
The key is absent on notifications not delivered via a subscription
stream (e.g. progress notifications for an in-flight request), which is
why it is optional here.

The value is the JSON-RPC ID of the `subscriptions/listen` request that
opened the stream.

types.name: NotificationParams
types.description: Common params for any notification.

types.name: PaginatedRequestParams
types.description: Common params for paginated requests.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the current pagination position.
If provided, the server should return results starting after this cursor.

fields.name: nextCursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
If present, there may be more results available.

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

types.name: ParseError
types.description: A JSON-RPC error indicating that invalid JSON was received by the server. This error is returned when the server cannot parse the JSON text of a message.

fields.name: code
fields.description: The error type that occurred.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Additional information about the error. The value of this member is defined by the sender (e.g. detailed error information, nested errors etc.).

fields.name: message
fields.description: A short description of the error. The message SHOULD be limited to a concise single sentence.

types.name: PrimitiveSchemaDefinition
types.description:
Restricted schema definitions that only allow primitive types
without nested objects or arrays.

types.name: ProgressNotification
types.description: An out-of-band notification used to inform the receiver of a progress update for a long-running request.

types.name: ProgressNotificationParams
types.description: Parameters for a {@link ProgressNotificationnotifications/progress} notification.

fields.name: message
fields.description: An optional message describing the current progress.

fields.name: progress
fields.description: The progress thus far. This should increase every time progress is made, even if the total is unknown.

fields.name: progressToken
fields.description: The progress token which was given in the initial request, used to associate this notification with the request that is proceeding.

fields.name: total
fields.description: Total number of items to process (or total progress required), if known.

types.name: ProgressToken
types.description: A progress token, used to associate progress notifications with the original request.

types.name: Prompt
types.description: A prompt or prompt template that the server offers.

fields.name: arguments
fields.description: A list of arguments to use for templating the prompt.

fields.name: description
fields.description: An optional description of what this prompt provides

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for {@link Tool},
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

types.name: PromptArgument
types.description: Describes an argument that a prompt can accept.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A human-readable description of the argument.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: required
fields.description: Whether this argument must be provided.

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for {@link Tool},
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

types.name: PromptListChangedNotification
types.description: An optional notification from the server to the client, informing it that the list of prompts it offers has changed. This is only delivered on a {@link SubscriptionsListenRequestsubscriptions/listen} stream when the client requested it via the `promptsListChanged` filter field.

types.name: PromptMessage
types.description:
Describes a message returned as part of a prompt.

This is similar to {@link SamplingMessage}, but also supports the embedding of
resources from the MCP server.

types.name: PromptReference
types.description: Identifies a prompt.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for {@link Tool},
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

types.name: ReadResourceRequest
types.description: Sent from the client to the server, to read a specific resource URI.

types.name: ReadResourceRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `resources/read` request.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of the resource. The URI can use any protocol; it is up to the server how to interpret it.

types.name: ReadResourceResult
types.description: The result returned by the server for a {@link ReadResourceRequestresources/read} request.

fields.name: cacheScope
fields.description:
Indicates the intended scope of the cached response, analogous to HTTP
`Cache-Control: public` vs `Cache-Control: private`.

- `"public"`: The response does not contain user-specific data. Any
  client or intermediary (e.g., shared gateway, caching proxy) MAY cache
  the response and serve it across authorization contexts.
- `"private"`: The response MAY be cached and reused only within the
  same authorization context. Caches MUST NOT be shared across
  authorization contexts (e.g., a different access token requires a
  different cache).

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

fields.name: ttlMs
fields.description:
A hint from the server indicating how long (in milliseconds) the
client MAY cache this response before re-fetching. Semantics are
analogous to HTTP Cache-Control max-age.

- If 0, The response SHOULD be considered immediately stale,
  The client MAY re-fetch every time the result is needed.
- If positive, the client SHOULD consider the result fresh for this many
  milliseconds after receiving the response.

types.name: ReadResourceResultResponse
types.description: A successful response from the server for a {@link ReadResourceRequestresources/read} request.

types.name: RequestId
types.description: A uniquely identifying ID for a request in JSON-RPC.

types.name: RequestMetaObject
types.description: Extends {@link MetaObject} with additional request-specific fields. All key naming rules from `MetaObject` apply.

fields.name: io.modelcontextprotocol/clientCapabilities
fields.description:
The client's capabilities for this specific request. Required.

Capabilities are declared per-request rather than once at initialization;
an empty object means the client supports no optional capabilities.
Servers MUST NOT infer capabilities from prior requests.

fields.name: io.modelcontextprotocol/clientInfo
fields.description:
Identifies the client software making the request. Clients SHOULD
include this field on every request unless specifically configured not
to do so.

The {@link Implementation} schema requires `name` and `version`; other
fields are optional.

The value is self-reported by the client and is not verified by the
protocol. It is intended for display, logging, and debugging. Servers
SHOULD NOT use it to change their behavior, and SHOULD NOT rely on it for
security decisions.

fields.name: io.modelcontextprotocol/logLevel
fields.description:
The desired log level for this request. Optional.

If absent, the server MUST NOT send any {@link LoggingMessageNotificationnotifications/message}
notifications for this request. The client opts in to log messages by
explicitly setting a level. Replaces the former `logging/setLevel` RPC.

fields.name: io.modelcontextprotocol/protocolVersion
fields.description:
The MCP Protocol Version being used for this request. Required.

For the HTTP transport, this value MUST match the `MCP-Protocol-Version`
header; otherwise the server MUST return a `400 Bad Request`. If the
server does not support the requested version, it MUST return an
{@link UnsupportedProtocolVersionError}.

fields.name: progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by {@link ProgressNotificationnotifications/progress}). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.

types.name: RequestParams
types.description: Common params for any request.

types.name: Resource
types.description: A known resource that the server is capable of reading.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
A description of what this resource represents.

This can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available resources. It can be thought of like a "hint" to the model.

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type of this resource, if known.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: size
fields.description:
The size of the raw resource content, in bytes (i.e., before base64 encoding or any tokenization), if known.

This can be used by Hosts to display file sizes and estimate context window usage.

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for {@link Tool},
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of this resource.

types.name: ResourceContents
types.description: The contents of a specific resource or sub-resource.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type of this resource, if known.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of this resource.

types.name: ResourceLink
types.description:
A resource that the server is capable of reading, included in a prompt or tool call result.

Note: resource links returned by tools are not guaranteed to appear in the results of {@link ListResourcesRequestresources/list} requests.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
A description of what this resource represents.

This can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available resources. It can be thought of like a "hint" to the model.

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type of this resource, if known.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: size
fields.description:
The size of the raw resource content, in bytes (i.e., before base64 encoding or any tokenization), if known.

This can be used by Hosts to display file sizes and estimate context window usage.

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for {@link Tool},
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of this resource.

types.name: ResourceListChangedNotification
types.description: An optional notification from the server to the client, informing it that the list of resources it can read from has changed. This is only delivered on a {@link SubscriptionsListenRequestsubscriptions/listen} stream when the client requested it via the `resourcesListChanged` filter field.

types.name: ResourceRequestParams
types.description: Common params for resource-related requests.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of the resource. The URI can use any protocol; it is up to the server how to interpret it.

types.name: ResourceTemplate
types.description: A template description for resources available on the server.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
A description of what this template is for.

This can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available resources. It can be thought of like a "hint" to the model.

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type for all resources that match this template. This should only be included if all resources matching this template have the same type.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for {@link Tool},
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

fields.name: uriTemplate
fields.description: A URI template (according to RFC 6570) that can be used to construct resource URIs.

types.name: ResourceTemplateReference
types.description: A reference to a resource or resource template definition.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI or URI template of the resource.

types.name: ResourceUpdatedNotification
types.description: A notification from the server to the client, informing it that a resource has changed and may need to be read again. This is only sent for resources the client opted in to via the `resourceSubscriptions` field of a {@link SubscriptionsListenRequestsubscriptions/listen} request.

types.name: ResourceUpdatedNotificationParams
types.description: Parameters for a `notifications/resources/updated` notification.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of the resource that has been updated. This might be a sub-resource of the one that the client actually subscribed to.

types.name: Result
types.description: Common result fields.

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

types.name: ResultMetaObject
types.description: Extends {@link MetaObject} with additional result-specific fields. All key naming rules from `MetaObject` apply.

fields.name: io.modelcontextprotocol/serverInfo
fields.description:
Identifies the server software producing the response. Servers SHOULD
include this field on every response unless specifically configured not
to do so.

The {@link Implementation} schema requires `name` and `version`; other
fields are optional.

The value is self-reported by the server and is not verified by the
protocol. It is intended for display, logging, and debugging. Clients
SHOULD NOT use it to change their behavior, and SHOULD NOT rely on it for
security decisions.

types.name: ResultType
types.description:
Indicates the type of a {@link Result} object, allowing the client to
determine how to parse the response.

complete - the request completed successfully and the result contains the final content.
input_required - the request requires additional input and the result contains an {@link InputRequiredResult} object with instructions for the client to provide additional input before retrying the original request.

types.name: Role
types.description: The sender or recipient of messages and data in a conversation.

types.name: Root
types.description: Represents a root directory or file that the server can operate on.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
An optional name for the root. This can be used to provide a human-readable
identifier for the root, which may be useful for display purposes or for
referencing the root in other parts of the application.

fields.name: uri
fields.description:
The URI identifying the root. This *must* start with `file://` for now.
This restriction may be relaxed in future versions of the protocol to allow
other URI schemes.

types.name: SamplingMessage
types.description: Describes a message issued to or received from an LLM API.

types.name: ServerCapabilities
types.description: Capabilities that a server may support. Known capabilities are defined here, in this schema, but this is not a closed set: any server can define its own, additional capabilities.

fields.name: completions
fields.description: Present if the server supports argument autocompletion suggestions.

fields.name: experimental
fields.description: Experimental, non-standard capabilities that the server supports.

fields.name: extensions
fields.description:
Optional MCP extensions that the server supports. Keys are extension identifiers
(e.g., "io.modelcontextprotocol/tasks"), and values are per-extension settings
objects. An empty object indicates support with no settings.

Keys MUST follow the {@link MetaObject`_meta` key naming rules}, with a
mandatory prefix.

fields.name: logging
fields.description: Present if the server supports sending log messages to the client.

fields.name: prompts
listChanged
fields.description: Whether this server supports notifications for changes to the prompt list.
Present if the server offers any prompt templates.

fields.name: resources
listChanged
fields.description: Whether this server supports notifications for changes to the resource list.

fields.name: subscribe
fields.description: Whether this server supports subscribing to resource updates.
Present if the server offers any resources to read.

fields.name: tools
listChanged
fields.description: Whether this server supports notifications for changes to the tool list.
Present if the server offers any tools to call.

types.name: SubscriptionFilter
types.description:
The set of notification types a client may opt in to on a
{@link SubscriptionsListenRequestsubscriptions/listen} request.

Each notification type is **opt-in**; the server **MUST NOT** send
notification types the client has not explicitly requested here.

fields.name: promptsListChanged
fields.description: If true, receive {@link PromptListChangedNotificationnotifications/prompts/list_changed}.

fields.name: resourceSubscriptions
fields.description:
Subscribe to {@link ResourceUpdatedNotificationnotifications/resources/updated} for these resource URIs.
Replaces the former `resources/subscribe` RPC.

fields.name: resourcesListChanged
fields.description: If true, receive {@link ResourceListChangedNotificationnotifications/resources/list_changed}.

fields.name: toolsListChanged
fields.description: If true, receive {@link ToolListChangedNotificationnotifications/tools/list_changed}.

types.name: SubscriptionsAcknowledgedNotification
types.description:
Sent by the server to acknowledge that a
{@link SubscriptionsListenRequestsubscriptions/listen} subscription has been
established and to report which notification types it agreed to honor.

This notification MUST be the first message the server sends carrying the
subscription's ID in `io.modelcontextprotocol/subscriptionId`. The server MUST
NOT send any notification on the subscription before acknowledging it. On
stdio, where every subscription shares one channel, this ordering is defined
per subscription ID and not per channel: messages belonging to other
subscriptions MAY be interleaved before it.

types.name: SubscriptionsAcknowledgedNotificationParams
types.description: Parameters for a {@link SubscriptionsAcknowledgedNotificationnotifications/subscriptions/acknowledged} notification.

fields.name: notifications
fields.description:
The subset of requested notification types the server agreed to honor.
Only includes notification types the server actually supports; if the
client requested an unsupported type (e.g., `promptsListChanged` when
the server has no prompts), it is omitted from this set.

types.name: SubscriptionsListenRequest
types.description:
Sent from the client to open a long-lived channel for receiving notifications
outside the context of a specific request. Replaces the previous HTTP GET
endpoint and ensures consistent behavior between HTTP and STDIO.

types.name: SubscriptionsListenRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a {@link SubscriptionsListenRequestsubscriptions/listen} request.

fields.name: notifications
fields.description:
The notifications the client opts in to on this stream. The server
**MUST NOT** send notification types the client has not explicitly
requested.

types.name: SubscriptionsListenResult
types.description:
The response to a {@link SubscriptionsListenRequestsubscriptions/listen}
request, signalling that the subscription has ended gracefully (for example,
during server shutdown). Because the listen stream is long-lived, this result
is sent only when the server tears the subscription down; an abrupt transport
close carries no response. The result body is otherwise empty.

fields.name: resultType
fields.description:
Indicates the type of the result, which allows the client to determine
how to parse the result object.

Servers implementing this protocol version MUST include this field.
For backward compatibility, when a client receives a result from a
server implementing an earlier protocol version (which does not include
`resultType`), the client MUST treat the absent field as `"complete"`.

types.name: SubscriptionsListenResultMeta
types.description:
Extends {@link ResultMetaObject} with the subscription-stream identifier carried by a
{@link SubscriptionsListenResult}. All key naming rules from `MetaObject` apply.

fields.name: io.modelcontextprotocol/serverInfo
fields.description:
Identifies the server software producing the response. Servers SHOULD
include this field on every response unless specifically configured not
to do so.

The {@link Implementation} schema requires `name` and `version`; other
fields are optional.

The value is self-reported by the server and is not verified by the
protocol. It is intended for display, logging, and debugging. Clients
SHOULD NOT use it to change their behavior, and SHOULD NOT rely on it for
security decisions.

fields.name: io.modelcontextprotocol/subscriptionId
fields.description:
Identifies the subscription stream this response closes, so the client can
correlate it with the originating subscription — mirroring the same key on
the stream's notifications. The value is the JSON-RPC ID of the
`subscriptions/listen` request that opened the stream (and equals this
response's `id`).

types.name: TextContent
types.description: Text provided to or from an LLM.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text content of the message.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type of this resource, if known.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text of the item. This must only be set if the item can actually be represented as text (not binary data).

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of this resource.

types.name: TitledMultiSelectEnumSchema
types.description: Schema for multiple-selection enumeration with display titles for each option.

fields.name: default
fields.description: Optional default value.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional description for the enum field.

fields.name: items
anyOf
const
fields.description: The constant enum value.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Display title for this option.
Array of enum options with values and display labels.
Schema for array items with enum options and display labels.

fields.name: maxItems
fields.description: Maximum number of items to select.

fields.name: minItems
fields.description: Minimum number of items to select.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Optional title for the enum field.

types.name: TitledSingleSelectEnumSchema
types.description: Schema for single-selection enumeration with display titles for each option.

fields.name: default
fields.description: Optional default value.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional description for the enum field.

fields.name: oneOf
const
fields.description: The enum value.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Display label for this option.
Array of enum options with values and display labels.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Optional title for the enum field.

types.name: Tool
types.description: Definition for a tool the client can call.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description:
Optional additional tool information.

Display name precedence order is: `title`, `annotations.title`, then `name`.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
A human-readable description of the tool.

This can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available tools. It can be thought of like a "hint" to the model.

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

fields.name: inputSchema
fields.description:
A JSON Schema object defining the expected parameters for the tool.

Tool arguments are always JSON objects, so `type: "object"` is required at the root.
Beyond that, any JSON Schema 2020-12 keyword may appear alongside `type` — including
composition keywords (`oneOf`, `anyOf`, `allOf`, `not`), conditional keywords
(`if`/`then`/`else`), reference keywords (`$ref`, `$defs`, `$anchor`), and any other
standard validation or annotation keywords.

Property schemas may carry an `x-mcp-header` annotation to mirror the
argument value into an HTTP header on the Streamable HTTP transport. See
the Streamable HTTP transport specification for the validity and
extraction rules.

Defaults to JSON Schema 2020-12 when no explicit `$schema` is provided.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: outputSchema
fields.description:
An optional JSON Schema object defining the structure of the tool's output returned in
the structuredContent field of a {@link CallToolResult}. This can be any valid JSON Schema 2020-12.

Defaults to JSON Schema 2020-12 when no explicit `$schema` is provided.

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for {@link Tool},
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

types.name: ToolAnnotations
types.description:
Additional properties describing a {@link Tool} to clients.

NOTE: all properties in `ToolAnnotations` are **hints**.
They are not guaranteed to provide a faithful description of
tool behavior (including descriptive properties like `title`).

Clients should never make tool use decisions based on `ToolAnnotations`
received from untrusted servers.

fields.name: destructiveHint
fields.description:
If true, the tool may perform destructive updates to its environment.
If false, the tool performs only additive updates.

(This property is meaningful only when `readOnlyHint == false`)

Default: true

fields.name: idempotentHint
fields.description:
If true, calling the tool repeatedly with the same arguments
will have no additional effect on its environment.

(This property is meaningful only when `readOnlyHint == false`)

Default: false

fields.name: openWorldHint
fields.description:
If true, this tool may interact with an "open world" of external
entities. If false, the tool's domain of interaction is closed.
For example, the world of a web search tool is open, whereas that
of a memory tool is not.

Default: true

fields.name: readOnlyHint
fields.description:
If true, the tool does not modify its environment.

Default: false

fields.name: title
fields.description: A human-readable title for the tool.

types.name: ToolChoice
types.description: Controls tool selection behavior for sampling requests.

fields.name: mode
fields.description:
Controls the tool use ability of the model:
- `"auto"`: Model decides whether to use tools (default)
- `"required"`: Model MUST use at least one tool before completing
- `"none"`: Model MUST NOT use any tools

types.name: ToolListChangedNotification
types.description: An optional notification from the server to the client, informing it that the list of tools it offers has changed. This is only delivered on a {@link SubscriptionsListenRequestsubscriptions/listen} stream when the client requested it via the `toolsListChanged` filter field.

types.name: ToolResultContent
types.description: The result of a tool use, provided by the user back to the assistant.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description:
Optional metadata about the tool result. Clients SHOULD preserve this field when
including tool results in subsequent sampling requests to enable caching optimizations.

fields.name: content
fields.description:
The unstructured result content of the tool use.

This has the same format as {@link CallToolResult.content} and can include text, images,
audio, resource links, and embedded resources.

fields.name: isError
fields.description:
Whether the tool use resulted in an error.

If true, the content typically describes the error that occurred.
Default: false

fields.name: structuredContent
fields.description:
An optional structured result value.

This can be any JSON value (object, array, string, number, boolean, or null).
If the tool defined an {@link Tool.outputSchema}, this SHOULD conform to that schema.

fields.name: toolUseId
fields.description:
The ID of the tool use this result corresponds to.

This MUST match the ID from a previous {@link ToolUseContent}.

types.name: ToolUseContent
types.description: A request from the assistant to call a tool.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description:
Optional metadata about the tool use. Clients SHOULD preserve this field when
including tool uses in subsequent sampling requests to enable caching optimizations.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
A unique identifier for this tool use.

This ID is used to match tool results to their corresponding tool uses.

fields.name: input
fields.description: The arguments to pass to the tool, conforming to the tool's input schema.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the tool to call.

types.name: UnsupportedProtocolVersionError
types.description:
Returned when the request's protocol version is unknown to the server or
unsupported (e.g., a known experimental or draft version the server has
chosen not to implement). For HTTP, the response status code MUST be
`400 Bad Request`.

fields.name: requested
fields.description: The protocol version that was requested by the client.

fields.name: supported
fields.description:
Protocol versions the server supports. The client should choose a
mutually supported version from this list and retry.

types.name: UntitledMultiSelectEnumSchema
types.description: Schema for multiple-selection enumeration without display titles for options.

fields.name: default
fields.description: Optional default value.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional description for the enum field.

fields.name: items
enum
fields.description: Array of enum values to choose from.
Schema for the array items.

fields.name: maxItems
fields.description: Maximum number of items to select.

fields.name: minItems
fields.description: Minimum number of items to select.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Optional title for the enum field.

types.name: UntitledSingleSelectEnumSchema
types.description: Schema for single-selection enumeration without display titles for options.

fields.name: default
fields.description: Optional default value.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional description for the enum field.

fields.name: enum
fields.description: Array of enum values to choose from.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Optional title for the enum field.

capabilities.name: elicitation
capabilities.description: Present if the client supports elicitation from the server.

capabilities.name: experimental
capabilities.description: Experimental, non-standard capabilities that the client supports.

capabilities.name: extensions
capabilities.description:
Optional MCP extensions that the client supports. Keys are extension identifiers
(e.g., "io.modelcontextprotocol/oauth-client-credentials"), and values are
per-extension settings objects. An empty object indicates support with no settings.

Keys MUST follow the {@link MetaObject`_meta` key naming rules}, with a
mandatory prefix.

capabilities.name: roots
capabilities.description: Present if the client supports listing roots.

capabilities.name: sampling
capabilities.description: Present if the client supports sampling from an LLM.

capabilities.name: completions
capabilities.description: Present if the server supports argument autocompletion suggestions.

capabilities.name: experimental
capabilities.description: Experimental, non-standard capabilities that the server supports.

capabilities.name: extensions
capabilities.description:
Optional MCP extensions that the server supports. Keys are extension identifiers
(e.g., "io.modelcontextprotocol/tasks"), and values are per-extension settings
objects. An empty object indicates support with no settings.

Keys MUST follow the {@link MetaObject`_meta` key naming rules}, with a
mandatory prefix.

capabilities.name: logging
capabilities.description: Present if the server supports sending log messages to the client.

capabilities.name: prompts
capabilities.description: Present if the server offers any prompt templates.

capabilities.name: resources
capabilities.description: Present if the server offers any resources to read.

capabilities.name: tools
capabilities.description: Present if the server offers any tools to call.
